name: Milestone Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured business lender offering SBA 7(a) and 504 loans up to $5M and equipment financing since 1988, with same-day approval and next-day funding.
description_long:
Milestone Bank has operated as a business lending institution since 1988, positioning itself as an established provider of commercial financing solutions. The company is FDIC-insured and maintains physical locations in Troy, Michigan and Salt Lake City, Utah, lending credibility through federal deposit insurance backing.

Milestone Bank specializes in two primary lending products: SBA business loans (7(a) and 504 programs) offering up to $5,000,000, and equipment financing ranging from $350,000 to $4,000,000. They advertise same-day approval with next-day funding capabilities and position themselves as handling documentation burden for applicants ("We Do the Heavy Lifting"). Equipment financing covers 12 specific industry verticals including manufacturing, construction, food & beverage, IT, automotive repair, and wireless technology.

The company differentiates itself through multi-decade experience (over 30 years serving business owners), established partnerships with resellers, distributors, and manufacturers, and industry-specific equipment financing solutions. They emphasize cash conservation through leasing rather than purchasing and provide dedicated SBA lending experts to guide loan selection between 504 (real estate) and 7(a) (business expansion) products.

However, Milestone Bank's profile shows limited transparency around pricing, interest rates, approval requirements, and processing timelines beyond "same-day approval" and "next-day funding" claims. The website lacks specific case studies, customer testimonials, or detailed qualification criteria. For equipment financing, minimum loan amounts ($350,000) exclude many small businesses, and no information is provided about personal credit requirements, debt-to-income ratios, or industry-specific restrictions.
services: SBA 504 business loans for commercial real estate purchases
Equipment leasing solutions for cash conservation
Business financing consultation with SBA lending experts
Bridge loan inquiries (mentioned in contact form)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits provide federal backing and consumer protection up to $250,000 per account
SBA loan amounts up to $5,000,000 accommodate significant business expansion and real estate purchases
Equipment financing available for 12 specific industries (manufacturing, construction, food & beverage, IT, automotive, wireless tech, etc.)
Advertises same-day approval and next-day funding for time-sensitive business needs
Company handles document gathering and signature collection (reduced applicant burden)
Over 30 years of lending experience with established partnerships across reseller and distributor networks
Two physical office locations (Michigan and Utah) with dedicated phone support and fax services
cons: Equipment financing minimum of $350,000 excludes small businesses and startups needing under $350K
Website lacks pricing transparency—no APR ranges, origination fees, or rate examples disclosed
No published approval criteria, credit score minimums, debt-to-income requirements, or qualification details
Limited customer testimonials or case studies to verify actual funding speed and service quality claims
Generic "same-day approval" and "next-day funding" claims lack specificity regarding conditions and exceptions

contact.address: 310 South Main St., Suite 900, Salt Lake City, UT 84101

best_for: Established businesses with revenue and credit history seeking $350K+ equipment purchases across defined industries
Companies needing SBA 7(a) loans for expansion or 504 loans for commercial real estate acquisition
Equipment resellers, distributors, and manufacturers with customers requiring equipment financing solutions
diagnosis: Milestone Bank is best suited for established small-to-mid-sized businesses with existing credit and revenue history seeking substantial equipment financing ($350K+) or SBA-backed business loans for expansion and real estate. The primary caveat is lack of transparency on rates, fees, and approval requirements—applicants must contact directly to understand true costs and eligibility, and minimum equipment loan amounts exclude many smaller operations.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 310 South Main St., Suite 900, Salt Lake City, UT 84101
